content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lester J. Marston 
Rapport & Marston 
200 Henry street 
P.O. Box 488 
Ukiah, CA 
95482 
Dear Mr. Marston: 
May 19, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-223 
This letter confirms the telephone advice which was given to 
you on May 5, 1989. 
You requested advice on behalf of Mr. Herbert 
Giese concerning application of the conflict-of-interest provi-
sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,)l to his duties as a 
member of the Willits city council. 
The following advice is based 
upon the facts provided in your letters of April II, 1989 and 
April 20, 1989. 
QUESTION 
Under the Act, must Mr. Giese disqualify himself from 
participating in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood 
Energy Corporation, since both he and the Harwoods employ 
Ms. Rowland? 
CONCLUSION 
Mr. Giese may participate in a decision regarding a use 
permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation. 
Based on the facts 
provided, neither Mr. Giese nor Giese Realty has a financial 
interest in Ms. Rowland. 
FACTS 
Mr. Herbert Giese is a member of the Willits City Council, 
and will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood 
1 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 窶｢ 
P.O. Box 807 窶｢ Sacramento CA 95804~0807 窶｢ (916) 322~5660 


File No. A-89-223 
Page 2 
Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the City 
of willits. 
Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. 
He has two contracts 
with Ms. Rita Rowland, a real estate agent. 
The first contract is 
a broker-agent independent contractor employment contract. 
Under 
this contract, Mr. Giese, a broker, employs Ms. Rowland, an agent, 
to list and sell real estate. 
If Ms. Rowland sells any property 
listed with Giese Realty, the commission is paid directly to 
Mr. Giese, who then pays Ms. Rowland a percentage of the commis-
sion. 
The second contract is a managing broker agreement. 
As of 
January 1, 1988, Ms. Rowland assumed the responsibility of manager 
of Giese Realty, managing all activities relating to real estate 
and real estate sales persons. 
As compensation for these duties, 
Ms. Rowland is paid 10 percent of the gross commissions received 
by Giese Realty. 
For the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has also been employed by 
Harwood Energy Corporation, which is owned by Morris and Bud 
Harwood. 
She is a real estate consultant and is paid a salary of 
$1,000 a month. 
She handles a variety of real estate matters, 
which include evaluating and communicating purchase offers to the 
Harwoods, reviewing and obtaining copies of documents, keeping 
current records on all the properties and updating the property 
taxes. 
In the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has not acted as the 
real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the Harwoods and 
therefore has not earned any commission income. 
The work that Ms. Rowland performs for the Harwoods is done 
primarily at their office, which is about five blocks from Giese 
Realty. 
However, on occasion she reviews documents and makes 
phone calls on behalf of the Harwoods from the Giese Realty of-
fice. 
All such calls are billed to the Harwoods. 
The Harwoods' properties are not listed with Giese Realty, 
and none of Giese's real estate agents have sold any of the 
Harwoods' properties within the last 12 months. 
None of the 
income that Ms. Rowland has received under her written employment 
contract with the Harwoods has been paid to Mr. Giese. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat-
ing in, or using his official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) 
A public official has a 
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable 
that the decision will have a material financial effect, 
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the 
official or a member of his immediate family or on, among other 
things: 


File No. A-89-223 
Page 3 
(a) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts 
and other than loans by a commercial lending 
institution in the regular course of business on 
terms available to the public without regard to 
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received 
by or promised to the public official within 12 
months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 
(Section 87103.) 
Mr. Giese may not participate in a decision if it is reason-
ably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect on any business entity in which he has an interest or on 
any person or entity which was a source of income to him within 
the 12 months preceding the decision. 
(Section 87103(a), (c) and 
(d) 窶｢ ) 
The question implicit in your letters concerns Ms. Rowland's 
dual employment relationship with both Mr. Giese and the Harwoods, 
and if this creates a conflict of interest which would prohibit 
Mr. Giese from voting on the Harwood Energy Corporation use 
permit. 
According to your April 20, 1989 letter, Ms. Rowland was 
not the real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the Harwoods, 
and none of the income that Ms. Rowland received from the Harwoods 
was paid to Mr. Giese. 
Therefore, the Harwoods are not a indirect 
source of income to Mr. Giese under section 87103(c). 
Furthermore, the Harwoods are also not a source of income to 
Mr. Giese since none of the Harwoods' properties are listed with 
Giese Realty and none of the Giese real estate agents have sold 
any of the Harwoods' properties within the past 12 months. 
We must then determine if Ms. Rowland is a source of income 
to Mr. Giese under the broker-agent independent contractor employ-
ment contract. 
Regulation 18704.3 defines sources of commission 
income for both real estate brokers and real estate agents. 
Regulation 18704.3(c) provides as follows: 


File No. A-89-223 
Page 4 
(c) 
The sources of commission income in a 
specific sale or similar transaction include for 
each of the following: 
* * * 
(2) 
A real estate broker: 
(A) 
The person the broker 
represents in the transaction; 
(B) 
If the broker receives a com-
mission from a transaction conducted by 
an agent working under the broker's 
auspices, the person represented by the 
agent; 
(C) 
Any brokerage business entity 
through which the broker conducts busi-
ness; and 
(D) 
Any person who receives a 
finder's or other referral fee for refer-
ring a party to the transaction to the 
broker, or who makes a referral pursuant 
to a contract with the broker. 
(3) 
A real estate agent: 
(A) 
The broker and brokerage busi-
ness entity under whose auspices the 
agent works; 
(B) 
The person the agent represents 
in the transaction; and 
(C) 
Any person who receives a 
finder's or other referral fee for refer-
ring a party to the transaction to the 
broker, or who makes a referral pursuant 
to a contract with the broker. 
According to the regulation, Mr. Giese is a source of income to 
Ms. Rowland, but she is not a source of income to him. 
Therefore, Ms. Rowland's dual employment relationship with 
both Mr. Giese and the Harwoods does not present Mr. Giese with a 
financial interest which would affect his participating in or mak-
ing any decisions regarding the Harwood Energy Corporation use 
permit. 


File No. A-89-223 
Page 5 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:JRS:plh 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804-0807 
RE: 
Request for Advice Letter 
Our File No. W-2.7 
Dear Ms. Griffiths: 
I am writing to you on behalf of my client, Herbert Giese, 
who is a member of the Willits City Council. 
On May 8, 1989, Mr. 
Geise will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood 
Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the 
City of Willits. 
Harwood Energy Corporation is owned by Morris 
and Bud Harwood. 
Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. 
In that capacity he 
contracts with independent real estate brokers. 
One of those 
brokers is Rita Rowland. 
Under Ms. Rowland's contract with Mr. Giese, she pays him 
10% of the gross profit that she receives from real estate sales 
made through Mr. Giese's office. 
Under her contract she is en-
titled to utilize Mr. Giese's office and offer property for sale 
under the listing of Giese Realty. 
Within the last 12 months Ms. Rowland has been employed by 
the Harwoods to assist them in a variety of real estate matters. 
These real estate matters have included anything from obtaining 
copies of documents such as deeds of trusts or foreclosure 
notices to things as simple as taking documents to the Harwoods 
for their signature. 
None of the work that Ms. Rowland has per-
formed on the Harwood's behalf has included real estate sales. 
Furthermore, Ms. Rowland has never paid Mr. Giese any money or 
compensation for any of the work that she has performed on behalf 
of the Harwoods. 
I am wr it ing to you now on behal f of Mr. Giese to request 
that you render an opinion to Mr. Giese as to whether or not he 
has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act which 
prohibits him from voting on the Harwood use permit. 
Since the 
vote on this item will take place on or about May 8th or 9th, I 


Diane M. Griffiths, April 11, 1989, Page 2 
would appreciate it if you could render an opinion on or before 
that date. 
If you need any additional assistance in order to act on 
this request, please do not hesitate to give me a call. 
LJM/le 
cc: Herb Giese 
Yours very truly, 
:::2'--
LESTER J. MARSTON 
City Attorney

Dear Mr. Marston: 
April 14, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-223 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform Act 
was received on April 13, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Jill Stecher an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 17, 1988 
Kathleen Modugno 
Friends of Coastal Open Space/ 
Yes on Proposition C 
P.O. Box 91840 
Santa Barbara, CA 
93190 
Re: 
88-223 
Dear Ms. Modugno: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 15, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
窶｢ 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

sections.question:
Under the Act, must Mr. Giese disqualify himself from 
participating in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood 
Energy Corporation, since both he and the Harwoods employ 
Ms. Rowland?
sections.conclusion:
Mr. Giese may participate in a decision regarding a use 
permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation. 
Based on the facts 
provided, neither Mr. Giese nor Giese Realty has a financial 
interest in Ms. Rowland.

to list and sell real estate. 
If Ms. Rowland sells any property

sections.analysis:
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat-
ing in, or using his official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) 
A public official has a 
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable 
that the decision will have a material financial effect, 
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the 
official or a member of his immediate family or on, among other 
things:  
(a) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts 
and other than loans by a commercial lending 
institution in the regular course of business on 
terms available to the public without regard to 
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received 
by or promised to the public official within 12 
months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 
(Section 87103.) 
Mr. Giese may not participate in a decision if it is reason-
ably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect on any business entity in which he has an interest or on 
any person or entity which was a source of income to him within 
the 12 months preceding the decision. 
(Section 87103(a), (c) and 
(d) 窶｢ ) 
The question implicit in your letters concerns Ms. Rowland's 
dual employment relationship with both Mr. Giese and the Harwoods, 
and if this creates a conflict of interest which would prohibit 
Mr. Giese from voting on the Harwood Energy Corporation use 
permit. 
According to your April 20, 1989 letter, Ms. Rowland was 
not the real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the Harwoods, 
and none of the income that Ms. Rowland received from the Harwoods 
was paid to Mr. Giese. 
Therefore, the Harwoods are not a indirect 
source of income to Mr. Giese under section 87103(c). 
Furthermore, the Harwoods are also not a source of income to 
Mr. Giese since none of the Harwoods' properties are listed with 
Giese Realty and none of the Giese real estate agents have sold 
any of the Harwoods' properties within the past 12 months. 
We must then determine if Ms. Rowland is a source of income 
to Mr. Giese under the broker-agent independent contractor employ-
ment contract. 
Regulation 18704.3 defines sources of commission 
income for both real estate brokers and real estate agents. 
Regulation 18704.3(c) provides as follows:  
(c) 
The sources of commission income in a 
specific sale or similar transaction include for 
each of the following:

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the Act, must Mr. Giese disqualify himself from 
participating in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood 
Energy Corporation, since both he and the Harwoods employ 
Ms. Rowland?

CONCLUSION: Mr. Giese may participate in a decision regarding a use 
permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation. 
Based on the facts 
provided, neither Mr. Giese nor Giese Realty has a financial 
interest in Ms. Rowland.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission Lester J. Marston Rapport & Marston 200 Henry street P.O. Box 488 Ukiah, CA 95482 Dear Mr. Marston: May 19, 1989 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-89-223 This letter confirms the telephone advice which was given to you on May 5, 1989. You requested advice on behalf of Mr. Herbert Giese concerning application of the conflict-of-interest provi- sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,)l to his duties as a member of the Willits city council. The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your letters of April II, 1989 and April 20, 1989. QUESTION Under the Act, must Mr. Giese disqualify himself from participating in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation, since both he and the Harwoods employ Ms. Rowland? CONCLUSION Mr. Giese may participate in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation. Based on the facts provided, neither Mr. Giese nor Giese Realty has a financial interest in Ms. Rowland. FACTS Mr. Herbert Giese is a member of the Willits City Council, and will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 窶｢ P.O. Box 807 窶｢ Sacramento CA 95804~0807 窶｢ (916) 322~5660 File No. A-89-223 Page 2 Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the City of willits. Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. He has two contracts with Ms. Rita Rowland, a real estate agent. The first contract is a broker-agent independent contractor employment contract. Under this contract, Mr. Giese, a broker, employs Ms. Rowland, an agent, to list and sell real estate. If Ms. Rowland sells any property listed with Giese Realty, the commission is paid directly to Mr. Giese, who then pays Ms. Rowland a percentage of the commis- sion. The second contract is a managing broker agreement. As of January 1, 1988, Ms. Rowland assumed the responsibility of manager of Giese Realty, managing all activities relating to real estate and real estate sales persons. As compensation for these duties, Ms. Rowland is paid 10 percent of the gross commissions received by Giese Realty. For the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has also been employed by Harwood Energy Corporation, which is owned by Morris and Bud Harwood. She is a real estate consultant and is paid a salary of $1,000 a month. She handles a variety of real estate matters, which include evaluating and communicating purchase offers to the Harwoods, reviewing and obtaining copies of documents, keeping current records on all the properties and updating the property taxes. In the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has not acted as the real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the
